{{Game | Name=Pac-Man Museum | Guid=3030-43368 | Deck=Pac-Man Museum is a digital compilation of many traditional Pac-Man titles, from his "humble beginnings" to later entries like Pac-Man Battle Royale. | ReleaseDate=2014-02-25 | Characters=Characters/Blinky,Characters/Clyde,Characters/Cylindria,Characters/Funky,Characters/Inky,Characters/Miru,Characters/Ms_Pac_Man,Characters/Pac_Man,Characters/Pac_Man_Jr,Characters/Pinky,Characters/Spiral_27456,Characters/Spunky_32207,Characters/Sue_4073 | Concepts=Concepts/Canceled_Ports,Concepts/Day_One_DLC,Concepts/Digital_Distribution,Concepts/Downloadable_Content,Concepts/E3_2013,Concepts/Eating,Concepts/Ghost,Concepts/Leaderboard,Concepts/Maze,Concepts/Power_Up,Concepts/Spherical_Protagonist,Concepts/Titular_Protagonist,Concepts/Xbox_Game_Pass,Concepts/Xbox_One_Backwards_Compatibility | Developers=Companies/Mineloader_Software_Co_Ltd,Companies/Namco | Franchises=Franchises/Namco_Museum,Franchises/Pac_Man | Genres=Genres/Compilation | Locations=Locations/Pac_Land | Objects=Objects/Apple,Objects/Bell,Objects/Cherry,Objects/Dot,Objects/Fruit,Objects/Galaxian_Flagship,Objects/Key,Objects/Melon,Objects/Orange,Objects/Power_Pellet,Objects/Pretzel,Objects/Race_Car,Objects/Strawberry,Objects/Trumpet | Platforms=Platforms/PC,Platforms/PlayStation_Network_PS3,Platforms/Xbox_360_Games_Store | Publishers=Companies/Bandai_Namco_Entertainment | Games=Games/Atari_Vault,Games/Mega_Man_Anniversary_Collection,Games/PacMax_Classics,Games/Sonics_Ultimate_Genesis_Collection,Games/Super_Mario_All_Stars | Themes= | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/8/87790/2604871-box_pmm.png }}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"2604871-box_pmm.png","path":"8\/87790\/","mime":"image\/png","sizes":"square_tiny,square_medium,scale_tiny,square_mini,square_small,scale_avatar,square_avatar,scale_medium,screen_medium,scale_large,scale_small,scale_super,screen_kubrick","caption":null},"background":[]}' />
==Overview== Pac-Man Museum is a game compilation developed and digitally published by [[Companies/Bandai_Namco_Entertainment|Bandai Namco Games]] for the [[Platforms/Xbox_360|Xbox 360]], [[Platforms/PlayStation_3|PlayStation 3]], and [[Platforms/PC|PC]] on February 25, 2014, with the Japanese 360/PS3 versions delayed on June 25, 2014. It was later [[Concepts/Removed_from_XBLA|delisted]] on July 20, 2020, with an [[Games/Pac_Man_Museum|enhanced version]] released in 2022.
It includes 10 games from the [[Franchises/Pac_Man|Pac-Man]] series, as well as a bonus award system called the "Stampbook" that can be used to unlock bonus figurines (most of which are from the 2013 cartoon series [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pac-Man_and_the_Ghostly_Adventures Pac-Man and the Ghostly Adventures]) for the main menu "Pac-Room".
The game was announced to be released on the [[Platforms/Wii_U|Wii U]] and [[Platforms/Nintendo_3DS|Nintendo 3DS]], but was cancelled.
==Game List== * [https://www.giantbomb.com/pac-man/3030-7624 Pac-Man] (arcade, 1980) * [[Games/Ms_Pac_Man|Ms. Pac-Man]] (arcade, 1982) - Offered as free [[Concepts/Downloadable_Content|DLC]] prior to March 31, 2014, changed to $4.99 afterwards. * [https://www.giantbomb.com/super-pac-man/3030-1559 Super Pac-Man] (arcade, 1982) * [https://www.giantbomb.com/pac-pal/3030-23313 Pac &amp; Pal] (arcade, 1983) * [https://www.giantbomb.com/pac-land/3030-8575 Pac-Land] (arcade, 1984) * [https://www.giantbomb.com/pac-mania/3030-861 Pac-Mania] (arcade, 1987) * [https://www.giantbomb.com/pac-attack/3030-13943 Pac-Attack] (Genesis, 1993) * [https://www.giantbomb.com/namco-museum-battle-collection/3030-31470 Pac-Man Arrangement] (PSP, 2005) * [https://www.giantbomb.com/pac-man-championship-edition/3030-19552 Pac-Man Championship Edition] (Xbox 360, 2007) * [https://www.giantbomb.com/pac-man-battle-royale/3030-32149 Pac-Man Battle Royale] (arcade, 2011)
==Trivia== * Pac-Man Championship Edition was originally not going to be included in the PS3 and 360 versions of the game. Along with the cancellation of the WiiU and 3DS versions, the title was instead included in all versions.

<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"3264063-box_ps.png","path":"8\/87790\/","mime":"image\/png","sizes":"scale_super,square_mini,screen_medium,scale_small,scale_avatar,scale_tiny,scale_large,screen_kubrick,square_small,scale_medium,square_avatar,square_medium,square_tiny","caption":null},"background":{"file":"2965174-dkklunmwsaa7eyt.jpg","path":"0\/1992\/","mime":"image\/jpeg","sizes":"scale_tiny,square_avatar,scale_super,scale_large,square_medium,screen_kubrick,square_tiny,screen_medium,square_mini,screen_kubrick_wide,scale_small,scale_medium,ignore_jpg_scale_medium,scale_avatar","caption":""}}' />
==Overview== Picross S is a puzzle game developed and digitally published by [[Companies/Jupiter_Corp|Jupiter]] for the [[Platforms/Nintendo_Switch|Nintendo Switch]] on September 28, 2017.
The first entry in the long-running [[Franchises/Picross|Picross]] series to be released for the Switch, Picross S is a successor to the [[Franchises/Picross_e|Picross e]] series, featuring 300 [[Concepts/Nonogram|nonogram]] puzzles in two modes: Picross or Mega Picross. It later received [[Franchises/Picross_S|multiple sequels]], each featuring a new set of puzzles while adding new features and/or game modes.
Along with optional assistance options, the game features local two-player multiplayer, allowing players to play both co-operatively (by solving the puzzle) or competitively (by tracking who fills in the most correct spaces).

<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"2480120-pingscreen.png","path":"16\/164924\/","mime":"image\/png","sizes":"square_small,square_tiny,square_medium,scale_small,square_avatar,square_mini,scale_avatar,scale_medium,screen_medium,scale_large,scale_tiny,scale_super,screen_kubrick","caption":""},"background":[]}' />
==Overview== Ping! revolves around a character of the same name, who is a small, green ball. His girlfriend, [[Characters/Pingella|Pingella]] has been kidnapped by the Boxx Empire, and he must battle their forces to save her.
==Gameplay== Players control Ping! with the arrow keys. The space bar drops "[[Objects/Coin_Bombs|Coin Bombs]]", which are small traps that can defeat enemies. Players can collect [[Objects/Diamond|diamonds]] to receive points.
Unlike [[Games/Chomperman|Chomperman]] (which released later that same year), Ping!'s take on the [[Games/Pac_Man|Pac-Man]] formula is more centered on puzzle-solving. Instead of simply clearing the [[Concepts/Maze|maze]] and dodging enemies, the player sometimes is required to complete a task in order to collect the level's diamonds or deal with unique circumstances within the level.
==Development== The idea for Ping! was developed after [[People/Steven_W_Hunt|Steven W. Hunt]] had read an article detailing that a [[Platforms/Jaguar|Jaguar]] game called "Pong 2000" was supposed to be in development, where Pong was an anthropomorphic ball in a platforming environment to save his girlfriend. After the game apparently had not been released, he took the idea and went forward with it. Initially there was supposed to be a "prequel" game called Ping (without the exclamation point), where players knocked the character between two Boxx Soldier paddles, like [[Games/Pong|Pong]]. Ping was scrapped after development on [[Games/Shadys_Poopong|Shady's Poopong]] began.
==Release== Ping! was released in mid-2005 as a downloadable title for PC. It was later included on the disc release "[[Games/CC_And_SH_Smash_Hits|CC andamp;amp;amp; SH Smash Hits]]" in 2006.

revision.comment: Request for correction of missing information.
revision.text:
{{Game |Guid=f1b26d92-21af-41b9-bdb7-ef14abeef707 |Name=PlayM2M |Deck=Online Free to Play MMORPG Game |ReleaseDateType=Full |ReleaseDate=2025-03-28 |Developers=Companies/Laniatus LLC |Genres=Genres/MMORPG |Platforms=Platforms/PC |Publishers=Companies/Laniatus LLC |Themes=Themes/Fantasy }} PlayM2M is a developed fantasy, mythological MMORPG developed and published by Laniatus LLC. The game uses a proprietary game engine. Its official early access date was March 28, 2025.
PlayM2M is an open-world, fantasy-style online role-playing game '''MMORPG''' in which players can choose from four character classes: Dorhan, Skogr, Petrun, and Wizard. Each class has two specializations that offer different combat styles for Player vs. Environment (PvE) and Player versus Player (PvP) modes.
The game features three selectable dynasties from which players can choose: the Rasan Dynasty, the Teven Dynasty, and the Solon Dynasty; players select one of these three at the start of the game. New players are introduced to the game through tutorial quests that cover basic mechanics such as movement controls, combat, equipment management, and mount usage.
